exa-web-search.description: Neural web search for market research and real-time data

context7.description: Live documentation lookup for libraries and frameworks

firecrawl.description: Web scraping for competitor pages, pricing, and market data

github.description: GitHub PRs, issues, and repo management

code-review-graph.description: Local code graph for blast-radius PR review, caller/test tracing, and token-efficient codebase exploration

_playwright_optional._note: Playwright is OPTIONAL in Cursor. If you have the Pencil extension installed, it already covers browser automation. Uncomment and rename this key to 'playwright' only if you need it without Pencil.
_playwright_optional.description: Browser automation — skip if using Pencil extension

_setup_instructions.cursor_ui: Go to Cursor → Settings → Features → MCP Servers. Add each server manually using the command and args above. Paste env var values in the Environment Variables section per server.
_setup_instructions.cursor_json: Alternatively, if Cursor supports a JSON config file, merge the mcpServers block above into your Cursor MCP config file.
_setup_instructions.api_keys: Set EXA_API_KEY, FIRECRAWL_API_KEY, and GITHUB_PAT in your shell profile (~/.zshrc or ~/.bashrc) before launching Cursor, or enter them directly in the Cursor MCP settings UI. code-review-graph requires no API key but does require Python 3.10+ and uvx or a local code-review-graph install.
_setup_instructions.recommendation: Keep 3-4 MCPs active in Cursor for normal work. Add code-review-graph during PR review, refactors, and codebase exploration.
